Plumbing  - The project is a new research facility on York University Campus with 2-storeys in building height. The Project will be connected to the existing Sherman Health Sciences Facility by walkways at Levels 1 and 2. Level 1 (basement) contain labs with restricted access that will be classified as a Group F, Division 3 major occupancy. Level 2 (first storey) will include consultation offices and meeting spaces. Level 3 will consist of offices and workstations. Levels 2 and 3 will be classified as Group D major occupancies.

There are currently 5 steel pedestrian bridges in poor condition providing access to Calumet Residence. Rather than repair and replace the bridges, YorkU plans to permanently close the bridges for use. The application to amend the Zoning By-law proposes two new buildings for the property at 333-351 King Street East & 200 Front Street East. The proposed development includes a 37-storey residential building (140 metres tall) on the west side of the site and a 28-storey office building (127 metres tall) in the centre of the site. The footprint of the new office building would replace some of the existing retail uses fronting on Front Street East. The existing Globe and Mail Centre, Coca-Cola/George Brown building and existing buildings fronting on King Street East would remain in place. The proposal also includes two privately owned publicly-accessible spaces fronting onto Front Street East, and a child care facility with outdoor space on the second floor of the proposed office building.

Zoning By Law Amendment and concurrent Draft Plan of Subdvision applications to create 13 blocks within the William Baker Neighbourhood. Planned as new inclusive, compact and complete community that is walkable and well served by transit and active transportation. The Neighbourhood is intended to be developed over phases with a broad range and mix of uses, built forms, densities, housing and unit types and tenures (including affordable and seniors options), all supported by an existing higher order transit and existing and planned community services and facilities. The District Plan for the William Baker Neighbourhood includes development blocks at the south end proposed for a mix of high/mid and low-rise residential as well as mixed use blocks at the intersection of Keele Street and Sheppard Avenue, to create a Neighbourhood Focus at that gateway to the community featuring commercial uses and parks, in a location well served by transit. Building heights in this area are proposed to range from 4 to 20 storeys. Residential blocks proposed in the north portion, which will be developed through subsequent phases, provide for similar heights in the range of 4 to 20 storeys, with potential for some taller elements near the Downsview Park subway/GO station hub. Of great importance to the future of the Neighbourhood is preservation and enhancement of the woodlot. It will be a central feature of the new community and further enhanced with additional parkland being added around its edges as contemplated by the Secondary Plan. Additionally, a park at the southern end of the Neighbourhood is proposed to feature a new pedestrian/cycling bridge that will provide a convenient link to Downsview Park to the south.

REVISED PROPOSAL: Revised Zoning By-Law Amendment Application and site plan application (23 139161 ESC 24 SA) is to permit the development of a 24-storey building with a 7-storey podium that steps down to the adjacent properties to the north and east. Feeley Group is proposing to redevelop the subject site with an 8-storey mixed-use building. The subject site is currently comprised of four semi-detached residential dwellings fronting the south side of Kingston Road and the west side of Blantyre Avenue. East Beach Developments Limited is proposing to demolish the existing dwellings to facilitate the future redevelopment of the subject site with an 8-storey mixed-use building containing a total gross floor area (¿GFA¿) of 4,342 square metres and a total of 58 dwelling unitsThe proposed building incorporates geo-thermal energy, and will have a total height of 26.4 metres, comprised of seven residential floors (Levels 1-7) and one shared mechanical-amenity floor (Level 8). A total of 58 residential dwelling units are proposed, including 4 live-work units at grade and 54 units above. Overall, the proposed building will have a total GFA of approximately 4,342 square metres, resulting in a proposed density of 4.37 times the area of the lot.

OPA and rezoning for amendments to modify the performance standards applicable to the mixed-use building to be located at 274-284 King Street West on the West Block, and on incorporating the minor variances approved for 270 King Street West on the East Block (File No. A0681/21TEY) into the amending by-law. No other changes to the existing planning approvals are proposed as part of this submission. Furthermore, the changes presented through the proposal are all interior and relate to the uses and their distribution within the building; no exterior changes to the building permissions are proposed through the applications.

The subject site has been developed as a single-storey commercial plaza comprised of four buildings with a total gross floor area of 4,877 square metres. The buildings have been recently completed and will be occupied by a mix of retail, restaurant and service commercial uses. The proposed Official Plan Amendment would redesignate the easterly portion of the subject lands from Core Employment Areas to General Employment Areas and would introduce a Site and Area Specific Policy (SASP) applicable to the subject site. The SASP would permit retail and service commercial uses across the entirety of the site, which is currently split designated as Core Employment Areas and General Employment Areas by the City of Toronto Official Plan. The SASP would also prohibit major retail developments of 6,000 square metres or more of gross floor area.
